reasoning: The unsafe verdict was based on a false positive in order.htm. The email obfuscation technique is legitimate anti-spam protection for the official sales@win-rar.com address. No code executes during makepkg, and all other files are benign documentation/configs.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen misidentified legitimate email obfuscation in order.htm as phishing due to domain confusion (win-rar.com is legitimate). Deepseek correctly recognized this as safe.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped auditing the binary tarball (rarlinux-x64-722.tar.gz) containing the actual executables. This is a critical gap since binaries pose the highest risk.
false_positives: order.htm (Qwen): Flagged legitimate anti-spam email obfuscation as phishing. win-rar.com is the official domain, not a fake.
false_negatives: Binary executables: Both models skipped reviewing the rar/unrar binaries in the tarball, leaving potential malware risks undetected.
re_audit_focus: rarlinux-x64-722.tar.gz (extract and audit binaries using strings/behavioral analysis)
learnings: File selection: Prioritize binaries/scripts over documentation. Implement binary analysis (strings extraction, heuristic scanning).
Prompt quality: Add training for common anti-spam patterns to reduce false positives.
Failure modes: Models confuse legitimate domains/obfuscation; over-index on non-executable files; skip critical binaries.
